extraction.one_line_summary: PHILIPSEN Jasper attacked on Moere de Panne straight in crosswinds, shed all sprinters except KOOIJ/LAMPAERT/FRISON, held off KOOIJ in final.
tactical_patterns: Alpecin pre-emptively drove echelon on Moere straight instead of protecting sprinter, dropped rival leadout trains before final lap.
Soudal-QuickStep's four-rider numerical advantage paralyzed their chase response, allowing Ballerini's pulling to shed Groenewegen/Demare without closing gap.
FRISON bridged solo in massive gear after initial split, suggesting reconnaissance riders can exploit hesitant chase dynamics.

rider_signals.PHILIPSEN Jasper: positive: did own echelon work despite being protected sprinter, revealing diesel engine capacity rare among pure fastmen
rider_signals.KOOIJ Olav: positive: survived Philipsen-driven echelon and nearly outsprinted him, confirming upgrade from stage race opportunist to Classics contender
rider_signals.GROENEWEGEN Dylan: negative: dropped when Ballerini increased pace after bridge, suggesting crosswind positioning deficiency not just bad luck
rider_signals.DEMARE Arnaud: negative: shed simultaneously with Groenewegen under same tempo increase, confirming declining ability to handle attritional racing
rider_signals.ACKERMANN Pascal: negative: did chase work then abandoned before finale, tactical error suggesting poor form judgment
rider_signals.FRISON Frederik: positive: executed high-risk solo bridge that weaker classics riders wouldn't attempt, podium potential in windy one-dayers
